issues.title: RFI list page lacks loading skeleton and error state rendering
issues.description: The RFIs page does not render skeleton loaders during data fetch or display inline error/retry UI when the query fails. Users see a blank table or flash of empty content. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §3 criterion 11 requires skeleton loaders matching table layout, and §Global requires no blank screens.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the main RFIs page component: find where the RFI data is fetched (useRFIs hook or useQuery call). BEFORE: the component renders the table immediately with no check for loading/error state, showing either nothing or a bare empty state during fetch. AFTER: (1) Add a loading guard: when isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader consisting of 5 rows of placeholder rectangles matching the table column widths (RFI #, Title, Status, Priority, Due Date, Days Open) using a pulsing opacity animation (opacity 0.3 to 0.7, 1.5s cycle). Use the existing theme borderRadius and colors.borderSubtle for skeleton blocks. (2) Add an error guard: when error is truthy, render an inline error card with the message 'Unable to load RFIs' and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). Do NOT show a full-page error. (3) Keep the existing empty state for when data loads successfully but is empty. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: RFI, Submittal, Change Order, and Punch List tables missing ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation
issues.description: Table rows lack role='row', cells lack role='cell', tables lack aria-label. Status badges have no aria-label. Clickable rows have no keyboard handler. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §Global Accessibility requires axe-core zero critical violations, keyboard navigable tab order, and aria-live for status changes.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the component that renders the RFI table: BEFORE: the <table> element has no aria-label, <tr> elements for data rows have no role or tabIndex, status badge spans have no aria-label, and clicking a row only works via onClick with no onKeyDown handler. AFTER: (1) Add aria-label='RFI Register' to the <table> element. (2) Add role='row' and tabIndex={0} to each data <tr>. (3) Add an onKeyDown handler to each data <tr> that triggers the row click action when Enter or Space is pressed: onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); handleRowClick(rfi.id); } }}. (4) Add aria-label to each status badge span, e.g. aria-label={`Status: ${rfi.status}`}. (5) Add aria-label to each priority badge, e.g. aria-label={`Priority: ${rfi.priority}`}. (6) Ensure the overdue badge has aria-label='Overdue'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Change Orders page missing three-tier PCO/COR/CO workflow and markup calculation
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §15 requires a three-tier workflow (PCO → COR → CO) with cost breakdown by labor/material/equipment/sub, configurable markup percentages, and 'promote' action from PCO to CO. The current page treats all change orders as a flat list with a single status field and no cost breakdown or markup calculation. This is a fundamental domain gap.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx, in the main ChangeOrders component: BEFORE: change orders display as a flat table with columns Number, Title, Status, Amount, and no distinction between PCO/COR/CO types. There is no cost breakdown, no markup calculation, and no promote action. AFTER: (1) Add filter tabs at the top: 'All', 'PCO', 'COR', 'CO' that filter by co_type field. (2) In the table, add a 'Type' column that shows a colored badge: PCO (amber), COR (blue), CO (green). (3) In the summary bar above the table, show three metrics: 'Approved COs' with total dollar amount (green), 'Pending CORs' with total (amber), 'Open PCOs' with total (gray). Calculate these by filtering the change orders array by co_type and status. (4) Add a 'Promote' button (visible only when co_type is 'pco' and status is 'pending') that changes co_type to 'cor'. Gate this behind a permission check if usePermissions is available. (5) In each row's amount display, show the total_cost formatted as currency. If total_cost is positive prefix with '+', if negative show in red. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Punch List page not responsive — table overflows on mobile viewport
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §8 requires camera-first mobile workflow, card layout on mobile, and swipe for before/after photos. Currently the punch list renders as a desktop table that overflows horizontally on screens below 768px with no card alternative. Touch targets are likely below 44px minimum.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/PunchList.tsx, in the main PunchList component where the table/list is rendered: BEFORE: a single <table> element renders all punch items regardless of viewport width. On mobile (<768px) the table overflows horizontally. There is no card-based alternative layout. AFTER: (1) Add a useMediaQuery or window.innerWidth check (or use CSS media queries via a className) to detect mobile viewport (<768px). (2) On mobile, replace the table with a card-based list where each punch item renders as a card showing: Title (bold, 16px), Location (muted, 14px), a colored status dot + status label, priority badge, assigned_to name, and due date. Each card should have min-height 72px and padding 16px for adequate touch targets (44px+). (3) Make each card clickable to open the detail view. (4) On desktop (>=768px), keep the existing table layout unchanged. (5) Add a CSS media query or conditional wrapper: on mobile, metric cards at the top should stack in a single column with 8px gap. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Submittal approval chain visualization missing — no stepper showing reviewer sequence
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §4 criterion 2 requires 'Approval chain: defined at creation. Each reviewer in sequence receives notification only when the previous reviewer approves.' and an 'Approval Chain Visualization: horizontal stepper showing each reviewer with their decision status.' The current page shows submittals as a flat list with a single status column and no reviewer chain or stepper UI.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Submittals.tsx, in the submittal detail view or the main component where a submittal's status is displayed: BEFORE: the submittal detail shows only a single status badge (e.g. 'Under Review') with no indication of which reviewer is current, who has already approved, or the total review chain. AFTER: (1) Add an ApprovalStepper component inline (can be defined in the same file). It renders a horizontal stepper using flexbox with steps: 'Submitted' → 'GC Review' → 'Architect Review' → 'Final'. Each step shows a circle (24px) connected by a line (2px height). Completed steps use colors.statusActive (green fill), current step uses colors.primary (orange fill with pulse animation), future steps use colors.borderSubtle (gray outline). Below each circle show the reviewer role label in 12px muted text. (2) Derive the current step from the submittal status: 'pending'→step 0, 'submitted'→step 1, 'review_in_progress'→step 2, 'approved'/'approved_as_noted'→step 3, 'rejected'→mark current step red. (3) Render this stepper above the submittal details section, with 16px margin bottom. (4) Add aria-label='Approval progress' to the stepper container and aria-current='step' to the active step. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's RFI workflow includes email reply from architects without login and ball-in-court tracking with color coding. SiteSync has BIC color coding in the API layer but the frontend does not render it visibly. Procore's submittal module shows a multi-party review chain with stamped reviewer decisions visible inline — SiteSync lacks this entirely. Fieldwire's punch list has a two-step verification workflow with photo comparison that SiteSync's spec demands but does not implement. The change order three-tier pipeline (PCO/COR/CO) with markup calculation is standard in Procore and completely absent in SiteSync's current UI. These are not edge features — they are table-stakes workflows that every GC expects.
summary: The core-workflows module covers RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, and Punch List — the daily workhorses of construction PM. The API layer is reasonably well-structured with typed endpoints, pagination, and error transformation, but the frontend pages are incomplete against the PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA. Critical gaps: no loading/error states on list pages (blank screens during fetch), zero ARIA attributes on tables (accessibility failure), the Change Orders page is missing the entire three-tier PCO/COR/CO workflow with markup calculations, the Punch List is not mobile-responsive (table overflow on mobile breaks the field-first mandate), and the Submittal page has no approval chain visualization despite the spec requiring a horizontal stepper. Data richness is low — pages rely on mock data with no Supabase wiring despite the P0 priority to connect real data. Real-time subscriptions are absent. AI integration is limited to a GenRFIResponse component that is not wired into the RFI creation or response flow. The five surgical fixes target the highest-impact gaps: loading/error states to prevent blank screens, ARIA attributes for accessibility compliance, the three-tier change order workflow for domain correctness, mobile card layout for punch list field use, and the submittal approval stepper for interaction completeness.
